interfaces.name: TableProcessor
interfaces.signalFlowRole: Lookup table step in signal path - used by table-based shaping modes (e.g. Curve)

interfaces.name: DisplayBufferSource
interfaces.signalFlowRole: skip - UI communication point, not in signal path

parameterGroups.label: Input Stage
parameterGroups.rationale: Controls applied to the signal before the shaping function: drive gain and DC bias

parameters.name: Drive
parameters.description: The distortion drive amount in decibels

modChain.name: Drive Modulation
modChain.description: Audio-rate modulation of the drive amount

parameters.name: Bias
parameters.description: Adds a DC offset before the shaping function for asymmetric distortion

parameterGroups.label: Shaping
parameterGroups.rationale: Core shaping parameters: mode selection and oversampling quality toggle

parameters.name: Mode
parameters.description: Selects the waveshaping function (populated dynamically from available shapers)

parameters.name: Oversampling
parameters.description: Enables 4x oversampling to reduce aliasing from the shaping function

gaps.question: What is the processing order in applyEffect() / renderVoice()? Specifically: where do drive gain, bias, shaping function, and oversampling sit relative to each other? Is there DC removal after the shaper?
gaps.context: Need to author accurate pseudo-code showing the full per-voice signal chain from input to output.

gaps.question: Does PolyshapeFX share the same shaping engine as ShapeFX (e.g. a common ShapeFXBase class or shared shaper functions)? Or is it a separate implementation?
gaps.context: Understanding code sharing determines whether ShapeFX exploration findings can be reused and whether the signal path topology is similar.

gaps.question: How is Drive (0-60 dB) applied? Is it converted to a linear gain multiplier applied before the shaper? Does the modulation chain multiply the Drive value before conversion, or modulate the linear gain?
gaps.context: Drive is the only modulatable parameter - users need to understand how modulation interacts with the dB-scaled drive.

gaps.question: How is Bias (0-1) applied? Is the DC offset added to the signal before the shaper? Is it removed after shaping?
gaps.context: Users need to know whether bias-induced DC offset persists in the output or is removed post-shaping.

gaps.question: What shaping functions are available for each Mode value (0-33)? Which modes use the TableProcessor lookup table? Are these the same modes as ShapeFX?
gaps.context: Users need to know what each mode does. The table-based modes are the TableProcessor interface usage.

gaps.question: Where does the TableProcessor lookup occur in the signal path? Is it the shaping function itself for table-based modes (e.g. Curve)?
gaps.context: Required since TableProcessor is listed as an interface - need to understand its role in the shaping pipeline.

gaps.question: Is oversampling a simple on/off 4x toggle (as the parameter suggests)? Does it introduce latency? What is the CPU cost impact per voice?
gaps.context: As a per-voice effect, oversampling cost scales with polyphony - users need to understand the performance/quality trade-off.

gaps.question: Is there a dry/wet mix in PolyshapeFX? ShapeFX has a Mix parameter but PolyshapeFX does not - is the output always 100% wet?
gaps.context: Absence of Mix parameter suggests always-wet output, but need to confirm there is no hardcoded mix behavior.

notes: PolyshapeFX is the per-voice (polyphonic) counterpart to ShapeFX (monophonic MasterEffect). It has a much simpler parameter set - only 4 parameters vs ShapeFX's 12. Notable differences from ShapeFX: no pre-shaper filters (HighPass/LowPass/BypassFilters), no separate Gain parameter (only Drive), single Bias instead of per-channel BiasLeft/BiasRight, binary oversampling toggle instead of variable factor, no Mix/Autogain/Reduce/LimitInput. The Drive parameter has a modulation chain (constrainer '*' = any modulator type) making it dynamically controllable per-voice. The Drive modulation mode is 'gain' which is the standard multiply pattern. The Oversampling parameter is typed as Button with items ['Off', 'On'], confirming it is a simple toggle. The Mode parameter range (0-33) matches ShapeFX exactly, suggesting shared shaping functions.
